title: Treatment Pathway Analysis
description: Treatment Pathway analysis describes the sequence of treatments a cohort receives over time. It shows what proportion of patients start on each drug, switch, combine, or discontinue — producing a Sankey diagram of treatment flows.
tips: Add all treatment-of-interest cohorts to see the full treatment landscape for a condition.
The combination window ('permitted gap') controls how overlapping exposures are treated as a combination.
Minimum cell count hides rare pathways for privacy — adjust this threshold in the settings.
Pathway results are exploratory — use Incidence Rate or Estimation for inferential analyses.
Sorting the Sankey by frequency highlights the most common clinical journeys.
